blocks.content: After lunch the hallway outside the cafeteria was bottlenecked — two hundred kids trying to remember where fourth period was.
I found my locker. When I turned around, he was right there.
I was heading out when he caught up.
He was at his locker two down from mine. I didn't connect it until he turned around.
He was the kid who'd told me Jonathan was looking for me. I remembered the face.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: I know. You're Michaels' boy. It's not a bad thing — it's just how introductions work in a place like this.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm still figuring out what I am in a place like this.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: That's the most honest thing I've heard all day. I've been doing the same thing since 7:55.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Three power structures. Running parallel. Nobody talks about how they overlap.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You've been thinking about this since the morning?

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: I think about everything since the morning. It's a problem.

blocks.text: is paying attention to the same things you are.

blocks.prompt: DeShawn reads rooms the way you do. That's either useful or exhausting.

options.label: "Jonathan's table is one. Daniel's another. Haven't placed the third."
"I just got here. Still taking it in."
"Three power structures on day one is a lot."
